zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Walker於1583年9月在英國賀特福爾巴爾多可受洗時，她的父親Augustine Walker34歲，母親Mrs. Augustine Walker27歲。她於1610年4月14日與Richard Warren在英國賀特福爾格亞特 阿維爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她於1623年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼。她去世於1673年10月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼享年90歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼普萊姆斯普萊姆斯布利亞爾 希爾 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Walker于1583年9月在英国賀特福爾巴爾多可受洗时，她的父亲Augustine Walker34岁，母亲Mrs. Augustine Walker27岁。她于1610年4月14日与Richard Warren在英国賀特福爾格亞特 阿維爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她于1623年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼。她去世于1673年10月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼享年90岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼普萊姆斯普萊姆斯布利亞爾 希爾 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Walker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly North and Midlands) and Scottish: occupational name for a fuller, from Middle English walker, Old English wealcere (an agent derivative of wealcan ‘to walk, tread’), ‘one who trampled cloth in a bath of lye or kneaded it, in order to strengthen it’. This was the regular term for the occupation during the Middle Ages in western and northern England. Compare Fuller and Tucker . As a Scottish surname it has also been used as a translation of Gaelic Mac an Fhucadair ‘son of the fuller’. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

History: The name was brought to North America from northern England and Scotland independently by many different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. Samuel Walker came to Lynn, MA, c. 1630; Philip Walker was in Rehoboth, MA, in or before 1643. The surname was also established in VA before 1650; a Thomas Walker, born in 1715 in King and Queen County, VA, was a physician, soldier, and explorer.

variants.name: Walcher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gunther, Hans, Horst, Klaus.
German: variant of   Walker   or   Walch  .

variants.name: Waker
meanings: either a nickname for a watchful person or an occupational name for a watchman, from Middle English waker ‘watchful, vigilant’ (Old English wacor) and the derived noun.
mostly an early Modern English and Scots variant of   Walker  .

variants.name: Fuller
meanings: English: occupational name for a dresser of cloth, from Middle English fuller ‘fuller of cloth’ (partly from Old English fullere, partly from Old French fouleor, foleur, Latin fullo). Raw cloth had to be fulled, i.e. scoured, cleansed, and thickened by beating or trampling it in water, a process also known as walking or tucking, hence the surnames Walker and Tucker alongside Fuller. These three terms and surnames are characteristic of different parts of England. In general, in Middle English, Fuller is southern and eastern, while Walker belongs to the west and north and Tucker is southwestern. Compare Fullen .
English: variant of Fullard with loss of -d.
German (Füller): occupational name for a person whose work involved filling, such as a dauber, or a nickname for a gourmand or glutton. Compare Filler .

variants.name: Walke
meanings: German: metonymic occupational name for a fuller, from Middle High German walke ‘fulling mill’. Compare Walker .

variants.name: Walck
meanings: German: variant of   Walker   or   Walch  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Walker was christened in September 1583, in Baldock, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Augustine Walker, was 34 and her mother, Mrs. Augustine Walker, was 27. She married Richard Warren on 14 April 1610, in Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters. She immigrated to Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America in 1623. She died in October 1673, in Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 90, and was buried in Burial Hill Cemetery, Plymouth, Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Walker a été baptisée en septembre 1583, à Baldock, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Augustine Walker, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Augustine Walker, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Warren le 14 avril 1610, à Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles. Elle a immigré au Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1623. Elle est décédée en octobre 1673, à Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 90 ans, et a été enterrée à Burial Hill Cemetery, Plymouth, Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Walker im September 1583 in Baldock, Hertfordshire, England getauft wurde, war ihr Vater Augustine Walker 34 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Augustine Walker 27. Sie hat am 14. April 1610 in Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, England Richard Warren geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie ist 1623 nach Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America eingewandert. Sie starb im Oktober 1673 im Alter von 90 Jahren in Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Burial Hill Cemetery, Plymouth, Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Walkerが1583年9月にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアボードックで洗礼を受けた時，父Augustine Walkerは34歳，母Mrs. Augustine Walkerは27歳であった。1610年4月14日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアグレアト アムウエルでRichard Warrenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1623年にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーに移住した。彼女は1673年10月にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーで90歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニープリモウスプリモウスブリアル ヒル セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Walker(이)가 1583년 9월 영국 허트퍼드실르 볼독에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Augustine Walker(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Augustine Walker(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1610년 4월 14일 영국 허트퍼드실르 그리트 아웰에서 Richard Warren(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1623년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이로 이민을 떠났다. 그녀는 1673년 10월 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이에서 90세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 플림우스 플림우스 버이앨 힐 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Walker foi batizada em setembro de 1583, em Baldock, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Augustine Walker, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Augustine Walker, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard Warren em 14 de abril de 1610, em Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela imigrou para o Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America em 1623. Ela faleceu em outubro de 1673, em Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, com 90 anos, e foi sepultada em Burial Hill Cemetery, Plymouth, Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.Hingham, Massachusetts Bay, British Colo: хингхам, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Walker была крещена сентябрь 1583 г. в местности балдокк, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Augustine Walker было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Augustine Walker, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Warren 14 апреля 1610 г. в местности грит амвелл, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей. Она иммигрировала в плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1623 году. Она умерла октябрь 1673 г. в местности плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 90 лет и была похоронена в местности буряль хилл кеметири, плимоут, плимоут, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Walker se bautizó en septiembre de 1583, en Baldock, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Augustine Walker, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Mrs. Augustine Walker, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Richard Warren el 14 de abril de 1610, en Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 5 hijas. Inmigró a Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America en 1623. Murió en octubre de 1673, en Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 90 años, y fue sepultada en Burial Hill Cemetery, Plymouth, Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
